 Implementation:

 For deserialize_packet:

   hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
   uint32_t packet_id;
   adaptor.read(&packet_id);

   std::string username;
   adaptor >> hexi::prefixed(username);

   uint32_t score_count;
   adaptor.read(&score_count);
   std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
   hexi::read_container(adaptor, scores, score_count);

   std::string metadata;
   adaptor >> hexi::null_terminated(metadata);

   // Skip remaining bytes
   adaptor.skip(adaptor.size());

 For extract_packet_info:

   hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
   uint32_t packet_id = *adaptor.span<uint32_t>();

   std::string_view username;
   adaptor >> hexi::prefixed(username);

   uint32_t score_count;
   adaptor.read(&score_count);
   adaptor.skip(score_count * sizeof(uint32_t));

   Then return PacketInfo{packet_id, username};

 However, note that the span function for the packet_id in extract_packet_info: we are using span<uint32_t>() which returns a span of one element. We then dereference it to get the value.

 But wait, the span function in buffer_adaptor is defined as:
   template<typename out_type = value_type>
   std::span<out_type> span(size_type count) requires contiguous<buf_type> { ... }

 We are using it for a single uint32_t, so we can do:
   uint32_t packet_id = *adaptor.span<uint32_t>();

 Alternatively, we can use:
   auto id_span = adaptor.span<uint32_t>();
   uint32_t packet_id = id_span[0];

 But the problem says "Use span() to get a view of the first 4 bytes (packet_id)".

 Let's write the code accordingly.

 However, note that the buffer_adaptor constructor takes a buffer and sets the write_ pointer to the buffer's size. But in our case, we are only reading.

 Also, note that the buffer_adaptor class is in the hexi namespace.

 We must include the necessary headers. The problem already includes <hexi/hexi.h>.

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstddef> // Include for std::byte

void skip_bytes_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::size_t bytes) {
    buffer.skip(bytes);
}

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    MessageHeader header;
    buffer.read(&header);
    buffer.skip(header.body_length);
    return header;
}
```</answer>
